title: Procedural animation systems pack — six small ECS systems for character motion
intent: Once GDP-008 ships, every bomber is a node tree (torso + head + armL + armR + legL + legR + accessories) under a single root. This story makes those nodes MOVE: idle bob, walk cycle (two-bone-IK legs + arm swing + hip sway), bomb-place arm reach, hit recoil, slapstick death tween (supersedes / merges with GDP-004 if 004 has not landed yet), and spring physics for dangly accessories. Six small ECS systems, each independently unit-testable, all writing to the same generated mesh node Transforms. Replaces any pre-rendered animation pipeline with ~300 LOC of code. Sets the bar for what the AGF Tween + IK + Spring primitives can produce without external tooling.
rationale: Source: `docs/game-design/characters-and-visual.md` §3.3 (six-systems breakdown) + the user-locked 'no Mixamo, procedural animation' decision on 2026-05-20. Depends on GDP-008 — the systems need the node-tree to mutate. Sizes well as a single sprint because each system is independent and small; can also be split across two sprints if dev wants to land idle+walk first and the situational ones (bomb-place / hit / death / spring) second. Slapstick death (GDP-004) overlaps with the death system here — if GDP-004 has already landed, this story refines/replaces; if not, this story subsumes it.
acceptanceHints: Depends on GDP-2026-05-20-008. If 008 has not landed, defer 009 until it has.

Coordinate with GDP-2026-05-20-004 (Slapstick death) — if 004 shipped first against the legacy capsule prefab, the death system here EXTENDS the tween to scatter limb nodes from the generator tree (full slapstick effect). If 004 has not shipped, this story's death system covers both the prefab and generator cases.

examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/anim/idle-animation-system.ts — sine bob on torso.position.y, period ~1.0 s, amplitude ~0.025 m (~2.5 cm at toy scale). Plus a slow ±0.1 rad head.rotation.y sway, period ~3 s. Disabled when alive=false.

examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/anim/walk-animation-system.ts — when GridMover.velocity > 0: legL / legR rotation.x opposing sine waves (±0.7 rad, frequency proportional to velocity), armL / armR opposing-to-legs swing (±0.5 rad), torso.rotation.y sway toward walk direction (±0.1 rad). Phase advances with travelled distance, not wall-clock — slow bot animates slowly. Smooth blend with idle when velocity drops to zero.

examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/anim/bomb-place-animation-system.ts — on PlaceBombRequest (BEFORE BombPlacementSystem consumes it), schedule a Tween on the chosen arm (closer arm to the bomb cell wins ties): rotation toward a 'drop' pose (rotation.x = -1.4 rad) over 0.18 s, hold 0.05 s, return to neutral over 0.12 s. Total ~0.35 s. Idle / walk systems back off on that arm while the tween is active.

examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/anim/hit-recoil-system.ts — on BlastEvent that overlaps a bomber's grid cell: schedule a Tween on torso.rotation.x by −0.4 rad over 0.1 s, return to 0 over 0.18 s. Independent of the death tween (a non-lethal blast still triggers hit-recoil; current rules make every blast lethal but the system is non-coupled so a future Shield power-up works).

examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/anim/death-animation-system.ts — on BomberStats.alive false-flip: launch the bomber root +1.2 m on Y over 0.18 s ease-out, rotate +2π around Z over the full death-tween (~0.5 s), drop -1.5 m over 0.32 s ease-in, despawn at the end. If the bomber's node tree has separable limbs (post-GDP-008 generator output), additionally scatter limbL / armR / legL / legR via spring-physics impulse so the body ragdolls visually.

examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/anim/spring-sway-system.ts — generic two-tap spring on any node tagged 'soft-attached' (accessories like antennae, fins, backpack). Drives node.rotation toward a target (which is offset by the parent's angular velocity) with k=18, damping=0.4. ONE system, runs over every tagged node, regardless of which character it belongs to.

All six systems must be schedulable in the existing fixedUpdate loop and must not write to gameplay components (BomberStats, GridMover, GridPosition, RoundState) — animation-only. The grep-the-diff test: no animation system imports BomberStats / GridMover writes.

Per-system unit tests under tests/unit/anim/: idle (torso y oscillates with the expected period), walk (legs move out-of-phase with arms when velocity > 0, freeze on velocity = 0), bomb-place (arm rotates and returns to neutral within budget), hit (torso recoils and returns), death (root launches, rotates, despawns), spring (settles to target within ~6 ticks given k/damping defaults).

Integration test (extension of the existing bot-vs-bot.test.ts): run 60 seconds of fixedUpdate with the full system stack — round resolves within the same budget as today; no NaN in any Transform value; no entity leaks (death-system cleans up its own bomber tween + spring nodes).

Performance budget: with the full stack active and 4 bombers + 4 accessories each, the animation systems combined should add < 1 ms per fixedUpdate on a normal laptop browser. If the budget breaks, spring-sway is the first system to throttle (run every other tick).

Out of scope (explicit): bomb-throw / bomb-kick animations (separate story, depends on GDP-003), face / eye animation (would require a facial-node generator in 008), procedural taunts and victory dances (could come with GDP-010's victory slot or as a follow-up).
